fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Colorado Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Colorado - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity colorado, how to transfer dietitian license to colorado, dietitian compact colorado, colorado dietitian endorsement application

board.name: State Board of Dietetics and Nutrition (New)
board.parent_dept: Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA), Division of Professions and Occupations
board.mailing_address:
Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies
Office of Professions and Occupations
1560 Broadway, Suite 1350
Denver, CO 80202

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: ~$200 initial registration fee + $39.50 CBI background check (Estimated for FY 2026-27)
quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Annual (Estimated) + 75 CPE hours per 5-year CDR cycle
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 75 continuing professional education (CPE) hours per 5-year CDR cycle

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: Starting September 1, 2026, Colorado will require a state license to practice or offer medical nutrition therapy. Licensed Dietitians must hold current CDR registration or meet equivalent examination and education requirements.

license_types.title: Licensed Nutritionist (LN)
license_types.description: Colorado's new law also creates a Licensed Nutritionist pathway, which will have a separate examination option (with a waiver period until September 1, 2028 for qualifying candidates).

steps.title: No State Licensure Required
steps.description: The State of Colorado does not regulate dietetics or nutrition services. Therefore, there are no state-mandated steps or an application process to practice as a dietitian or nutritionist within Colorado.

steps.title: Professional Certification (Optional)
steps.description: While not required by the state, many professionals pursue national certifications such as the Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) credential from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to demonstrate competency and enhance professional standing.

steps.title: Employer Requirements
steps.description: Employers in Colorado may still require specific qualifications, certifications (like RDN), education, and experience for dietitian and nutritionist positions, even without state licensure.

steps.title: Local Regulations (Check with Municipality)
steps.description: It is advisable to check with local county or city governments for any specific local business licenses or regulations that might apply, although state-level professional licensure is not applicable.

documents.detail: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Colorado Bureau of Investigation background check, processed at a DORA-approved provider.
Verification of at least 1,000 hours of supervised practice from an ACEND-accredited program.

provisional_text: Colorado's new Board of Dietetics and Nutrition may establish provisions for temporary practice during the transition period. Check the DORA website as regulations are finalized.
reciprocity_text: Details on reciprocity or endorsement will be established by the new Board of Dietetics and Nutrition under DORA regulations. Active CDR registration will serve as the primary qualification for state licensure.

faqs.question: When does Colorado's dietitian license requirement take effect?
faqs.answer: The new licensure requirement takes effect on September 1, 2026. After this date, no person may practice or offer medical nutrition therapy without a state license.

faqs.question: How much will a Colorado dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The estimated initial registration fee is approximately $200 for FY 2026-27, plus a $39.50 CBI background check. Annual renewal is estimated between $125 and $180.

faqs.question: Do I need a license before September 1, 2026?
faqs.answer: No. Before September 1, 2026, Colorado does not mandate state licensure. The CDR credential (RD/RDN) remains the recognized standard until the new law takes effect.

faqs.question: Does Colorado have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Colorado is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Colorado?
faqs.answer: This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.

reciprocity.notes: Details on reciprocity or endorsement will be established by the new Board of Dietetics and Nutrition under DORA regulations. Active CDR registration will serve as the primary qualification for state licensure.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Colorado
hero.dek: Dietitians and Nutritionists are currently not licensed by the State of Colorado. There is no state application portal or registration system for these professions.

official_links.registration_steps: There is no state-level online application system or registration process as dietitians and nutritionists are not licensed by the State of Colorado.
